messages.content:
# altair_charts
Professional data visualization with Altair/Vega-Lite — chart selection, design system, color rules, and artifact ops

# Data Visualization — Design System & Rules

All charting constants and helpers are **pre-imported** in every `run_python` call.
Do NOT redefine or import them.

**CRITICAL — apply to every chart without exception:**
- NEVER call `.interactive()` — disabled in sandbox, crashes execution
- NEVER use `alt.selection_single()`, `alt.selection_multi()`, or `.add_selection()` — these were **removed in Altair 5**. Use `alt.selection_point()` / `alt.selection_interval()` and `.add_params()` instead. But prefer NO selections at all — the templates below don't need them.
- NEVER use pie charts — always use donut for part-of-whole
- ALWAYS include tooltips with formatted numeric values
- ALWAYS call `gs_theme(chart, title)` before saving — applies the dark theme
- ALWAYS call `save_artifact(chart, "Descriptive Title")` at the end
- ALL chart code, data query, and save_artifact() must be in ONE `run_python` call

---

## 1. Design Language

### Color Palette

| Constant | Hex | Role |
|---|---|---|
| `PRIMARY` | `#3d7be8` | Sapphire — primary series, main line |
| `ACCENT_TEAL` | `#2eb89a` | Teal — second series |
| `ACCENT_AMBER` | `#e8a820` | Amber — third series, annotations, callouts |
| `ACCENT_ROSE` | `#e05c7c` | Rose — fourth series |
| `ACCENT_PURPLE` | `#9b5de5` | Purple — fifth series |
| `ACCENT_CYAN` | `#38bdf8` | Cyan — sixth series |
| `POSITIVE` | `#22c55e` | Green — gains, above benchmark |
| `NEGATIVE` | `#f43f5e` | Red — losses, below benchmark |
| `NEUTRAL` | `#5c6278` | Steel — baselines, reference lines |
| `CAT_PALETTE` | 6-color list | `[PRIMARY, ACCENT_TEAL, ACCENT_AMBER, ACCENT_ROSE, ACCENT_PURPLE, ACCENT_CYAN]` |

### Canvas Sizes

| Constant | Dimensions | Use For |
|---|---|---|
| `W_STANDARD, H_STANDARD` | 640 × 400 | Default — most charts |
| `W_WIDE, H_WIDE` | 720 × 405 | Time series, multi-series |
| `W_COMPACT, H_COMPACT` | 400 × 300 | Small panels, narrow columns |
| `W_SQUARE, H_SQUARE` | 400 × 400 | Donut, radial |

### Typography & Marks

| Constant | Value | Use For |
|---|---|---|
| `LINE_WIDTH` | 2 | Line stroke width |
| `AREA_OPACITY` | 0.10 | Area fill under line |
| `BAR_CORNER_RADIUS` | 3 | Rounded bar caps |
| `BAR_OPACITY` | 0.85 | Bar fill opacity |
| `POINT_SIZE` | 50 | Point mark size |
| `POINT_FILL` | `"#0d1117"` | Point center fill (dark) |
| `POINT_STROKE_W` | 2 | Point stroke width |
| `SCATTER_SIZE` | 60 | Scatter point size |
| `SCATTER_OPACITY` | 0.75 | Scatter opacity |
| `DONUT_INNER` | 80 | Donut inner radius |
| `DONUT_OUTER` | 150 | Donut outer radius |

---

## 2. Chart Type Selection

Pick the chart type first, then load the matching sub-skill for templates.

| Data Shape | Best Chart | Sub-Skill |
|---|---|---|
| Single metric over time | Connected-dot line + area | `altair_charts/line_chart` |
| Multiple metrics over time (≤5 series) | Multi-series line | `altair_charts/line_chart` |
| Category comparison, ≤7 cats | Vertical bar, sorted descending | `altair_charts/bar_chart` |
| Category comparison, >7 cats or long labels | Horizontal bar, sorted descending | `altair_charts/bar_chart` |
| Values span positive AND negative | Diverging bar (green/red) | `altair_charts/bar_chart` |
| Proportional / part-of-whole | **Donut** (NEVER pie) | `altair_charts/donut_chart` |
| Two-variable relationship | Scatter + optional regression | `altair_charts/scatter_chart` |
| Distribution, frequency, spread | Histogram or KDE | `altair_charts/distribution` |
| Multiple groups × multiple categories | Grouped bar or heatmap | `altair_charts/advanced` |
| Threshold / benchmark reference | Add rule layer to any chart | `altair_charts/advanced` |
| Cumulative change / waterfall | Waterfall bar | `altair_charts/advanced` |

---

## 3. Color Assignment Rules

| Situation | Use |
|---|---|
| Single series | `PRIMARY` |
| Two series | `PRIMARY` + `ACCENT_TEAL` |
| Three+ series | `CAT_PALETTE[:N]` — never exceed 6 |
| Positive values | `POSITIVE` (green `#22c55e`) |
| Negative values | `NEGATIVE` (red `#f43f5e`) |
| Reference / baseline | `NEUTRAL` with `strokeDash=[4, 3]` |
| Annotations, callouts, peak labels | `ACCENT_AMBER` — highest contrast on dark bg |

Rules:
- Do NOT use `PRIMARY` for diverging charts — use `POSITIVE`/`NEGATIVE`
- Max 6 categorical colors — `CAT_PALETTE[:N]`
- Avoid mixing `CAT_PALETTE` with `POSITIVE`/`NEGATIVE` in the same chart

---

## 4. Formatting Rules

| Data Type | Format String |
|---|---|
| Percentage / rate | `",.2f"` or `",.1f"` |
| Currency (thousands) | `"$,.0f"` |
| Large counts | `",.0f"` |
| Dates (yearly) | `"%Y"` |
| Dates (monthly) | `"%b %Y"` |
| Dates (daily) | `"%b %d, %Y"` |

- NEVER `format=None` on numeric tooltip values
- Label angles: 0° for ≤5 cats, −30° for 6–10, −45° for 10+
- Suppress axis title when the field name is self-explanatory: `title=None`

---

## 5. Theming

`gs_theme(chart, title, width=W_STANDARD, height=H_STANDARD)` — always call
before saving. It applies the dark background, grid, font, and legend styling.

```python
chart = gs_theme(chart, "Chart Title")                         # standard size
chart = gs_theme(chart, "Wide Chart", width=W_WIDE)           # wide time series
chart = gs_theme(chart, "Square Chart", width=W_SQUARE, height=H_SQUARE)  # donut
```

---

## 6. Artifact Operations

### Save a new chart
```python
chart = gs_theme(chart, "Descriptive Title")
save_artifact(chart, "Descriptive Title")
```

### Update an existing chart (refinement)
Use `update_artifact()` — NOT `save_artifact()` — when the user asks to change
an existing chart. Pass the Altair object directly; do NOT call `.to_dict()` first.

```python
chart = gs_theme(chart, "Chart Title")
update_artifact("ARTIFACT_ID_HERE", chart)   # panel refreshes immediately
```

Wrong patterns to avoid:
```python
# ❌ save_artifact creates a NEW artifact — wrong for refinement
save_artifact(chart, title)

# ❌ .to_dict() returns a dict; update_artifact can accept it, but .to_json()
#    on a dict crashes — pass the chart object directly instead
spec = chart.to_dict()
update_artifact(id, spec.to_json())   # AttributeError
```

---

## 7. Loading Sub-Skills

After loading this skill, immediately load the sub-skill that matches the chart
type from the table in Section 2. Each sub-skill contains complete, ready-to-run
code templates.

```python
load_skill("altair_charts/bar_chart")      # vertical/horizontal/diverging bar
load_skill("altair_charts/line_chart")     # time series and trends
load_skill("altair_charts/donut_chart")    # composition / part-of-whole
load_skill("altair_charts/scatter_chart")  # correlation
load_skill("altair_charts/distribution")   # histogram, KDE, box plot
load_skill("altair_charts/advanced")       # reference lines, grouped, heatmap
```

# scatter_chart
Scatter plots, bubble charts, and correlation with regression line

# Scatter & Bubble Charts — Templates

Use for: correlations, two-variable relationships, cross-sectional comparisons.
Add a regression line to show the overall trend.

---

## Template A — Scatter + Regression Line

Best for: correlation between two numeric variables.

```python
import altair as alt

df = _db.execute("""
    SELECT x_col, y_col, label
    FROM your_table
""").df()

scatter = alt.Chart(df).mark_point(
    filled=True,
    color=PRIMARY,
    size=SCATTER_SIZE,
    opacity=SCATTER_OPACITY,
).encode(
    x=alt.X('x_col:Q', axis=alt.Axis(title='X Variable')),
    y=alt.Y('y_col:Q', axis=alt.Axis(title='Y Variable')),
    tooltip=[
        alt.Tooltip('label:N', title='Label'),
        alt.Tooltip('x_col:Q', title='X', format=',.2f'),
        alt.Tooltip('y_col:Q', title='Y', format=',.2f'),
    ]
)

regression = scatter.transform_regression(
    'x_col', 'y_col'
).mark_line(
    color=ACCENT_AMBER, strokeWidth=1.5, strokeDash=[4, 3]
)

chart = gs_theme(scatter + regression, "X vs Y Correlation")
save_artifact(chart, "X vs Y Correlation")
```

---

## Template B — Scatter Colored by Category

Best for: showing how groups cluster or separate in two-dimensional space.

```python
import altair as alt

df = _db.execute("""
    SELECT x_col, y_col, category, label
    FROM your_table
""").df()

n_cats = df['category'].nunique()

chart = alt.Chart(df).mark_point(
    filled=True, size=SCATTER_SIZE, opacity=SCATTER_OPACITY
).encode(
    x=alt.X('x_col:Q', axis=alt.Axis(title='X Variable')),
    y=alt.Y('y_col:Q', axis=alt.Axis(title='Y Variable')),
    color=alt.Color('category:N',
        scale=alt.Scale(range=CAT_PALETTE[:n_cats]),
        legend=alt.Legend(title=None)),
    tooltip=[
        alt.Tooltip('label:N', title='Name'),
        alt.Tooltip('category:N', title='Category'),
        alt.Tooltip('x_col:Q', title='X', format=',.2f'),
        alt.Tooltip('y_col:Q', title='Y', format=',.2f'),
    ]
)

chart = gs_theme(chart, "Category Scatter")
save_artifact(chart, "Category Scatter")
```

---

## Template C — Bubble Chart (size-encoded third dimension)

Best for: showing a third variable via point size (e.g., GDP × unemployment × population).

```python
import altair as alt

df = _db.execute("""
    SELECT x_col, y_col, size_col, label
    FROM your_table
""").df()

chart = alt.Chart(df).mark_point(
    filled=True, color=PRIMARY, opacity=SCATTER_OPACITY
).encode(
    x=alt.X('x_col:Q', axis=alt.Axis(title='X Variable')),
    y=alt.Y('y_col:Q', axis=alt.Axis(title='Y Variable')),
    size=alt.Size('size_col:Q',
        scale=alt.Scale(range=[50, 1000]),
        legend=alt.Legend(title='Size Metric')),
    tooltip=[
        alt.Tooltip('label:N', title='Name'),
        alt.Tooltip('x_col:Q', title='X', format=',.2f'),
        alt.Tooltip('y_col:Q', title='Y', format=',.2f'),
        alt.Tooltip('size_col:Q', title='Size', format=',.0f'),
    ]
)

chart = gs_theme(chart, "Bubble Chart")
save_artifact(chart, "Bubble Chart")
```

---

## Rules

- Always label axes clearly — field names alone are often not enough
- Use `transform_regression` only when correlation is the point of the chart
- For >500 points, reduce opacity to 0.4–0.5 to handle overplotting
- Use `ACCENT_AMBER` for regression line — contrasts well against `PRIMARY` points
- If points overlap heavily, consider a hexbin or histogram instead

---
**NEXT STEP:** Adapt the template above to the user's request. Call `run_python` with the complete script. Remember: `gs_theme`, `save_artifact`, `PRIMARY`, `CAT_PALETTE`, `_db`, and all constants are pre-imported — no imports needed. Use `:Q` for numbers, `:T` for dates, `:N` for categories. Do NOT call `.interactive()`.
